step_by_step_thinking:
Oil Red O staining is typically utilized to visualize lipid droplets within cells, which would be present after fixation methods that preserve cellular structure without altering lipids. The common fixatives used include formalin-fixed specimens as they provide good preservation of cell morphology while allowing visualization of neutral fats stained with Oil Red O.
My answer: C My reasoning aligns closely with standard protocols where Oil Red O staining specifically requires fixing agents like formalin to maintain cellular integrity suitable for visualizing intracellular lipid stores accurately. Therefore, option C (Formalin fixed specimen) best fits this requirement based on established histological practices.

Therefore, option C (Formalin fixed specimen) best fits this requirement based on established histological practices. Answer: C.
